title: tephrosia guayameoensis
intro: Tephrosia guayameoensis, also known as the Guayameo bean, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as wounds and eczema. Tephrosia guayameoensis is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal compounds to be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
